bullets.text: Seven people, including a senior police officer, were killed in an Israeli strike on a police post in north Gaza. Israel's military stated the strike targeted "terrorists."
A new US Ebola patient has arrived in Germany for treatment, while two drugs are being trialled in the Democratic Republic of Congo in a program set up just six weeks after the outbreak was declared.
Nigeria's army claims to have killed over 300 bandits in the north-western state of Zamfara, with vigilantes also reportedly participating in the fight.

There are concerns over a loophole in Victorian laws that allows unproven charges against councillors to trigger automatic stand-down provisions, potentially halting council operations across the state.
Australia's aged care regulator is investigating numerous complaints about providers charging residents for services they cannot use or understand, including Foxtel and wine.
Two people have died in a collision between a car and a truck on the McIvor Highway at Heathcote, north of Melbourne.
IBM's shares have dropped 25% due to customers shifting their spending towards AI, with CEO Arvind Krishna admitting the company 'faltered' as clients prioritized servers and storage for AI.
Major Wall Street banks like JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, Citigroup, and BofA have reported record earnings, driven by a stock trading boom fueled by the AI frenzy and the SpaceX IPO.
New York has become the first US state to implement a one-year moratorium on new data center construction, signed by Governor Kathy Hochul, amidst growing concerns over infrastructure demands.
